Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Paris Olympics 2024: Team SA in action on Saturday, 27 July

The Paris Olympics 2024 officially bursts into life on Saturday following Friday night’s opening ceremony.

TheParis Olympics 2024officially bursts into life on Saturday following Friday night’sopening ceremony.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section for free-to-read content

However, there has been some action in the build-up and one of that was rugby sevens where Team SA’s men are now one win away from a medal.

At the Stade de France later on Saturday, Team SA come up against hosts France in a mouth-watering semi-final. Should they win, they’ll be in the shootout for gold with either Fiji or Australia. Lose to France and Team SA will take on the losers of Fiji and Australia for the bronze medal.

In total there areeightsports involving Team SA on Saturday.

Skateboarding has been postponed until Monday due to the weather.

Follow all the live updatesHEREas the day unfolds.

All times SA

BADMINTONWomen’s singles, Group H (14:50):Johanita Scholtz vs Kim-Ga Eun (Kor)Note:Scholtz is ranked No 43 in the world and Eun is ranked No 13

EQUESTRIANEventing, Individual dressage (09:30-15:00):Alexander Peternell

HOCKEYMen’s Pool A (12:45):Team SA vs Netherlands

JUDOWomen’s 48kg elimination, round of 32 (10:24):Geronay Whitebooi vs Jacqueline Solis (ARG)Note:Whitebooi is ranked No 39 in the world and Solis is ranked No 63

ROWINGWomen’s single scull, heats (10:12):Paige BadenhorstHow it works:There are 6 heats of 5 competitors. The first 3 in each heat progress to the quarter-finals. The remainder head to the repechages

RUGBY SEVENSMen’s semi-final (15:30):Team SA vs FranceMen’s bronze (19:00):TBCMen’s final (19:45):TBC

SKATEBOARDING (Postponed to Monday due to the weather)Men’s street prelims:Brandon ValjaloMen’s street final:Brandon Valjalo TBC

SURFINGMen’s first round, heats (from 19:00):Jordy Smith and Matt McGillivrayWomen’s first round, heats (from 23:48):Sarah Baum

SWIMMINGWomen’s 100m butterfly, heats (11:08):Erin GallagherWomen’s 100m butterfly, semi-finals (20:30):Erin Gallagher TBC
